answer: Salivary glands are glands located within the mucous membranes of the mouth that constantly secrete saliva into the oral cavity to keep the mouth moist. There are six salivary glands: two parotid glands located in the cheeks anterior to the ears, two sublingual glands located beneath the tongue, and two submandibular glands located just beneath the mandible.
